2024-06-24.abstract: France is facing deep anticolonial resentment across Africa, but has found a partner in Rwanda, which is embracing French influence despite years of acrimony.

2024-06-24.abstract: The party that has dominated British politics through crisis after crisis could secure its lowest share of seats in Parliament in a century.

2024-06-24.title: The Deadly Flooding Tearing Through Southern China, in Photos
2024-06-24.abstract: The city of Meizhou reported a “once-in-a-century” flood, with at least 38 people dying in one county alone.

2024-06-25.title: Zelensky Removes a Top General Amid Criticism of Excessive Casualties
2024-06-25.abstract: The announcement by Ukraine’s president on Monday came hours after a scathing social media post implicitly accused the general of “killing more Ukrainian soldiers than any Russian general.”

2024-06-25.title: 2 Russian Women Put on a Play. Then the State Came for Them.
2024-06-25.abstract: The prosecution of a prominent playwright and a director in Russia over their work is a chilling sign of increased repression, cultural figures say.

2024-06-25.title: An Israeli panel warns Netanyahu over corruption case, and other news.
China’s Lunar Craft Returns to Earth
2024-06-25.abstract: Chang’e-6 parachuted down to an area of Inner Mongolia, carrying with it soil samples from the far side of the moon.

2024-06-25.title: Israeli Military Must Draft Ultra-Orthodox Jews, Supreme Court Rules
2024-06-25.abstract: The court ruled there was no legal justification for the ultra-Orthodox exemption from service, a decision that threatened to split Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu’s wartime government.

2024-06-26.title: Julian Assange Pleads Guilty to Espionage, Securing His Freedom
2024-06-26.abstract: The WikiLeaks founder, who entered the plea in a U.S. courtroom in Saipan in the Western Pacific, arrived home in Australia on Wednesday night.

2024-06-26.title: Russia Opens Secret Trial of U.S. Reporter Accused of Espionage
2024-06-26.abstract: Evan Gershkovich of The Wall Street Journal has endured 15 months in prison by reading letters and Russian classics, while the authorities have not publicly offered any evidence that he was a spy.

2024-06-26.title: Guess Who’s Back: A Trump Ally and Brexiteer Disrupts the U.K., Again
2024-06-26.abstract: With his populist, anti-immigration campaign, Nigel Farage is once more shaking up Britain’s politics. Can he finally win a seat in the national Parliament?

2024-06-26.title: China’s Anguished Debate: Do Its Children Have a Crime Problem?
2024-06-26.abstract: China has been considered relatively progressive on juvenile justice. But several high-profile killings have prompted calls for the law to come down more harshly on minors.

2024-06-26.title: Rogue to Victim: What Australia Sees in Julian Assange
2024-06-26.abstract: Broad support for his release seems to have grown more out of resentment of his treatment by the U.S. justice system than concerns about press protections.

2024-06-27.title: North Korea Says It Tested Multiple-Warhead Missile Technology
2024-06-27.abstract: The announcement, coming days after Vladimir V. Putin’s visit to Pyongyang, suggests an ambitious attempt to upgrade the North’s nuclear arsenal.

2024-06-27.title: The King and Keir: Is Charles About to Get a Prime Minister He Likes?
2024-06-27.abstract: The British monarch is constitutionally barred from any role in politics. But experts say that Charles and the leader of the Labour Party, Keir Starmer, have much in common.

2024-06-27.title: From Hacker to Hunted Figure, the Polarizing Legacy of Julian Assange
2024-06-27.abstract: The co-founder of WikiLeaks was a heroic crusader for truth to many people for publishing government secrets. To others, he was a reckless leaker endangering lives.

2024-06-27.title: Ahead of Election, Iranian Voters Say, ‘We Have Been Going Backward’
2024-06-27.abstract: Iranians say they have little faith their votes in Friday’s presidential election will improve their lives, and many are planning to sit it out.

2024-06-27.title: Shahjahan Bhuiya, Bangladeshi Executioner Turned TikTok Star, Is Dead
2024-06-27.abstract: He hanged high-profile inmates in exchange for a reduction in his own robbery and murder sentences, and became a social media sensation after his release.

2024-06-28.title: Part of Roof Collapses at India’s Busiest Airport After Heavy Rains
2024-06-28.abstract: At least one person was killed when parts of the roof caved in and crushed vehicles at Indira Gandhi airport in New Delhi, according to an official. All domestic departures were suspended.

2024-06-28.title: Why Britain’s Murky Election Betting Scandal Is Causing Outrage
2024-06-28.abstract: The embattled Conservative Party is embroiled in investigations over whether some of its own staff members used insider knowledge to bet on the timing of the general election.

2024-06-28.title: Iran’s Presidential Candidates: Who Are They?
2024-06-28.abstract: Four of the six candidates selected by officials to run in Iran’s special presidential election are still in the running after the president died in a helicopter crash last month.

2024-06-28.title: With Voter Discontent High, Turnout Appears to Plummet in Iran
2024-06-28.abstract: The latest polling suggested Dr. Masoud Pezeshkian, a reformist candidate, was in the lead, but not enough to avoid a runoff.

2024-06-28.title: No Appendix, No Problem. Australia’s Remote Doctors Tell All.
2024-06-28.abstract: Being the only physician around for hundreds of miles isn’t always bad. “It’s quite liberating to be like, ‘I’m the best person here for the job’,” said one doctor.

2024-06-29.title: ‘The Whole Country Is Falling to Bits’: Britain’s Young Voters Are Frustrated
2024-06-29.abstract: Many young people in the northern English cities of Liverpool and Manchester say they feel disillusioned by politics.

2024-06-29.title: Years Later, Philippines Reckons With Duterte’s Brutal Drug War
2024-06-29.abstract: A president’s vow to fight drugs unleashed violence and fostered a culture of impunity. But the crimes are finally getting a look, including from the International Criminal Court.

2024-06-29.title: Defendants Acquitted in Panama Papers Money-Laundering Trial
2024-06-29.abstract: The case focused on the law firm at the heart of the 2016 scandal, in which leaked documents revealed a vast network of offshore tax havens.

2024-06-29.title: The Digital World Is a Powder Keg. Julian Assange Lit the Fuse.
2024-06-29.abstract: In his brazen quest for total transparency, the WikiLeaks founder paved the way for a world in which no secret is safe and no institution trusted.

2024-06-29.title: Reformist Reaches Runoff in Iran’s Presidential Election
2024-06-29.abstract: Candidates in the campaign to fill the seat of President Ebrahim Raisi, who died in a helicopter crash, showed notable candor about the country’s mounting problems.

2024-06-30.title: Turnout Is High in France’s Snap Elections
2024-06-30.abstract: Participation was unusually strong as voters headed to the polls for a parliamentary election that could put the country on a new course. Here’s what to watch for.

2024-06-30.title: In the Basque Country, Muted Cheers for Spain’s Soccer Team
2024-06-30.abstract: The region has long seen itself as distinct from its country and disinterested in the national team. Can a Euro 2024 squad studded with Basque stars turn heads?

2024-06-30.title: Four Takeaways From Iran’s Presidential Election
2024-06-30.abstract: The results from Friday’s election, which drew record-low turnout for a presidential race, will force a runoff on July 5 between a reformist and an ultraconservative.

2024-06-30.title: The World’s Next Big Drag Queen Is Brazilian
2024-06-30.abstract: Pabllo Vittar has become an A-list pop star and L.G.B.T.Q. activist in Brazil. Can she conquer the world?

2024-06-30.title: India Wins Cricket World Cup, Stamping Its Domination of the Sport
2024-06-30.abstract: In India, cricket has become immensely profitable and a destination for the world’s best players. But a tournament victory had eluded it for many years.
